bead.title: Implement point-in-time rollback for collections (FEAT-023)
bead.description: Implement RollbackCollectionRequest { collection_id, since_timestamp: DateTime<Utc>, dry_run: bool } in axon-api/src/request.rs and RollbackCollectionResponse { compensating_writes: Vec<EntityId>, conflicts: Vec<EntityId> } in response.rs. Handler: scan audit log for all mutations to the collection after since_timestamp; for each affected entity, find its state at since_timestamp (the last audit entry at or before the cutoff); issue compensating writes via the existing update/delete paths. Entities that did not exist at the cutoff (created after since_timestamp) must be DELETED by the rollback — they appear in compensating_writes as delete operations. Conflicts: entities modified since their rollback target version (by a concurrent write after the rollback was initiated) are reported in conflicts list. All compensating writes share a single transaction_id with operation=rollback in audit metadata. Out of scope: cross-collection point-in-time rollback.
bead.acceptance: RollbackCollectionRequest with a past timestamp undoes all mutations after that point; compensating writes produce audit entries with operation=rollback; dry_run=true returns planned writes without committing; entities with OCC conflicts appear in conflicts field, not silently skipped; empty result when no mutations exist after the timestamp; NEW ENTITY TEST: entity E created AFTER the cutoff timestamp is DELETED by the rollback — E appears in compensating_writes as a delete operation (not an update to prior state, since it had no prior state)
